v172Q190R2ProductionActivePathHardening.title: Production Active Path Hardening
notes: Q190R2 was created after direct Q190R1 zip inspection found active production route present but physical legacy surface/comment/fallback history still present.
This phase hardens only the active production path: CSS inlining and raw insert-token commit rejection.
It does not claim full MathLive premium font/caret/Android PASS; Flutter analyze and Android retest remain mandatory.

v172Q190R3ProductionCommandBusCourt.title: Production Command Bus Court
notes: Q190R3 was created after Q190R2 still lacked a deterministic command-bus court to identify where Android key freezes occur.
It does not add user-facing debug UI and does not expand the public production bridge API.

It traces Flutter key tap, Dart dispatch start/sent/failed/timeout, JS bridge command receipt, insert commit/fail, queue flush, notifyFlutterState, focus/caret refresh, and clear/delete/set outcomes through production state metadata.
It does not claim Android PASS, premium symbol PASS, caret PASS, font bundle closure, or physical legacy cleanup.

v172Q190R4ProductionBridgeQueueFocusRepa.title: Production Bridge Queue Focus Repair
notes: Q190R4 was created after Q190R3 established command-bus tracing but active production currentStateJson still used runJavaScriptReturningResult readback.
The production currentStateJson path now pushes notifyFlutterState and returns cached channel state, avoiding Android returning-result dependency on the active production path.
Clear is treated as a priority reset for the Dart production command tail and JS pending queue.

Template insert-control payloads containing #0/#?/#@ are no longer converted to document-LaTeX fallback when native MathLive insert does not commit; the command fails loudly in state trace instead of painting half/raw templates.
This phase does not claim Flutter analyze/test/run PASS, Android PASS, premium font/symbol PASS, caret PASS, or physical legacy cleanup.

v172Q190R5TemplatePayloadSurgicalRepair.title: Template Payload Surgical Repair
notes: Q190R5 converts production WebView payloads from historical #0/#?/#@ insert-control tokens into explicit MathLive document placeholders before dispatch.
The JS production bridge repeats the conversion as a hard gate and blocks empty/raw-token document payloads from committing.

Q190R4 raw-token commit rejection remains in place; Q190R5 changes the production payload so critical structural templates can commit as document placeholder LaTeX instead of falling through as half/raw templates.
This phase does not claim Flutter analyze/test/run PASS, Android PASS, premium font/symbol PASS, caret PASS, or physical legacy cleanup.
